b237c158380e.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
b237c158380e.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
b237c158380e.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

787cb02f4061.title: Identify and fix the recurring guest complaint
787cb02f4061.description: At 3.5/5 on Google, something specific is landing badly. Read the 5 most recent negative reviews, identify the common thread, and fix it at source. The rating won't move until the root cause does.
787cb02f4061.management_implication: Below 4.0, Google actively suppresses your venue in search results.

64a4084a8026.title: Build review volume — you lack statistical credibility
64a4084a8026.description: Only 12 Google reviews. A single bad review moves your average visibly. Encourage satisfied customers to leave reviews.

cf32e185bf51.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
cf32e185bf51.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

0f5af5645e12.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
0f5af5645e12.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
0f5af5645e12.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

b86ba1fcdc5b.title: Ranked #3 of 4 locally — peers are ahead
b86ba1fcdc5b.description: At percentile 33.3, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

9a19a1a3a503.title: Guest experience trailing — investigate what peers do differently
9a19a1a3a503.description: You trail peers by 1.4 points on experience. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.
